nameMeanings.name: Tulloch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Orkney and Shetland): habitational name from any of various places called with Gaelic tulach ‘hillock, mound’, such as Tulloch in Dingwall (Ross and Cromarty) and Tough (Aberdeenshire).

variants.name: Tullis
meanings: Scottish (mainly Fife): probably a habitational name from the lands of Tullois (Fife). The placename appears to be from a Scots plural form of a word borrowed from Gaelic tulach ‘hillock’; compare Tulloch , Tullie .

variants.name: Tullock
meanings: Scottish (Shetland and Orkney): variant of   Tulloch  .

variants.name: Tough
meanings: Scottish (eastern): variant of Tulloch . In Scotland it is pronounced tyookh.
English: nickname from Middle English to(u)gh(e), tow(e) ‘strong, vigorous, stubborn’ (Old English tōh).

variants.name: Touch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Cambodian Sok, Bunthoeun, Chhom, Chhum, Choun, Eng, Heng, Hoeun, Koeun, Kosal, Samoeun, Savath, Somaly, Soun, Thoeun, Kao, Kon, Kong, Lim, Lun, Sen, Muon, Nguon, Phal, Phan, Pho, Than, Thy, Ry, Then.
Scottish (Argyll and Stirlingshire): variant of   Tulloch  .

variants.name: Tellock
meanings: Perhaps also German, of Slavic origin.

nameMeanings.name: Janet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

variants.name: Jannet
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

en.lifeSummary: Janet Tulloch was born on 29 July 1469, in Balblair, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland, United Kingdom. She married Walter Ross 1st of Shandwick in Balblair, Ross and Cromarty, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died on 10 June 1531, in her hometown, at the age of 61.

ko.lifeSummary: Janet Tulloch(은)는 1469년 7월 29일 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 바블레이르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 바블레이르에서 Walter Ross 1st of Shandwick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1531년 6월 10일 그녀의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.
